bronze_bull.name: Bronze Bull Idol
bronze_bull.lore: A small bronze figure of a bull, worn smooth by centuries of prayer. King Minos was given such a bull by Poseidon — and his refusal to sacrifice it at the sacred waters cursed his bloodline forever. The idol still smells faintly of sea-foam.

ariadnes_thread.name: Ariadne's Thread
ariadnes_thread.lore: A spool of shimmering golden thread. Ariadne gave this to Theseus so he could navigate the labyrinth and escape after slaying the Minotaur. It reveals all hidden paths.

eye_of_graeae.name: Eye of the Graeae
eye_of_graeae.lore: A single milky eye, cold as sea-stone. The three Grey Sisters — Deino, Enyo, and Pemphredo — shared this one eye between them. Perseus stole it and refused to return it until they revealed the secret of slaying Medusa. It still seems to watch you, even from your pack. The gods rewarded his cunning at a sacred place.

cats_footstep.name: Sound of a Cat's Footstep
cats_footstep.lore: A tiny glass vial that makes no sound when shaken. The dwarves of Svartalfheim harvested the silence of a cat's paw to forge Gleipnir, the binding that held Fenrir.

womans_beard.name: Roots of a Woman's Beard
womans_beard.lore: Fine filaments of something that shouldn't exist. The dwarves used them because they are impossible — and impossible things make the strongest chains.

mountain_root.name: Root of a Mountain
mountain_root.lore: A gnarled tendril of grey stone that pulses faintly with geologic patience. Mountains have roots that reach to the earth's core. The dwarves knew where to dig.

fish_breath.name: Breath of a Fish
fish_breath.lore: A sealed bubble of impossibility — air exhaled by a creature that has no lungs. It smells of deep water and cold currents.

bird_spittle.name: Spittle of a Bird
bird_spittle.lore: A single drop of moisture from a creature with no salivary glands. It glistens on its own, independent of any light source.

bear_sinew.name: Sinew of a Bear's Sensitivity
bear_sinew.lore: A thread of something between nerve and tendon, harvested from the concept of a bear's awareness. It twitches occasionally, sensing things you cannot.

gleipnir.name: Gleipnir
gleipnir.lore: A ribbon as thin as silk and as light as nothing. It cannot be broken by any force in the Nine Realms. The dwarves of Svartalfheim forged it from six impossible things, and it held the Wolf of Ragnarok until the end of days. It whispers of binding.

leather_scrap.name: leather scrap
leather_scrap.lore: Useless scrap left over from leather-working. Too small for armor, too stiff for bandages. You can't imagine why anyone would keep this.

seal_of_wrath.name: Seal of Wrath
seal_of_wrath.lore: A blood-red sigil torn from the first guardian of the Abyss. It burns with infernal heat and smells of brimstone. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_pestilence.name: Seal of Pestilence
seal_of_pestilence.lore: A sickly green sigil that weeps a foul ichor. The air around it hums with plague. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_famine.name: Seal of Famine
seal_of_famine.lore: A withered sigil the color of dead grain. Holding it makes you feel hollow, as though you have not eaten in days. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_war.name: Seal of War
seal_of_war.lore: A crimson sigil etched with crossed swords. It vibrates with the memory of a thousand battlefields. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_death.name: Seal of Death
seal_of_death.lore: A pale sigil cold as the grave. Your shadow darkens when you hold it. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_earthquake.name: Seal of the Earthquake
seal_of_earthquake.lore: A cracked sigil that trembles in your hand. The stone beneath your feet groans when you carry it. One of seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

seal_of_silence.name: Seal of Silence
seal_of_silence.lore: A sigil of pale silver that absorbs all sound around it. In its presence, even your breathing is muffled. The last and most terrible of the seven seals that hold the Pit closed.

scales_of_michael.name: Scales of Michael
scales_of_michael.lore: The golden scales of the Archangel Michael, Weigher of Souls and Prince of the Heavenly Host. In the final battle against the Destroyer, the Host answers the call of these scales. For every locust of the Abyss, an angel descends.

cursed_lodestone.name: Cursed Lodestone
cursed_lodestone.lore: A massive black stone that drinks the light around it. You accepted it willingly to save a knight's dying wife. It weighs twenty pounds and cannot be put down — the curse binds it to whoever takes it freely. Only a scroll of Remove Curse can break the bond.

sealed_dispatch.name: Sealed Dispatch
sealed_dispatch.lore: An iron-bound scroll case sealed with the sigil of the City Watch. It contains troop positions for an ongoing siege — thousands of lives depend on its delivery. A dying courier asked you to carry it to the surface. It is heavy and useless to you, but it is a promise.
